meta.framework: Next.js 15 + React 19 + Tailwind CSS v4 + Framer Motion + Spline 3D
meta.design_language: Apple 2026 Liquid Glass / visionOS Spatial UI
meta.aesthetic: rich, layered, graphically advanced — NOT minimal

always: Spline 3D scene as living background layer
layered depth with soft ambient glow shadows
calm soothing color palette — NOT tech blue

spline_3d.scene_role: full-viewport background layer, z-index: 0, pointer-events: none
spline_3d.scene_concept: slow-rotating soft mesh orbs — lavender, sage, warm pearl — with particle field. Depth of field blur at edges.
spline_3d.fallback: CSS gradient-mesh animated with @keyframes for browsers without WebGL
spline_3d.performance: load lazily after LCP, suspend with React Suspense, reduce motion respected
spline_3d.embed_pattern: <Spline scene='...' style={{ position:'fixed', inset:0, zIndex:0, opacity:0.6 }} />

glass_panel_spec.glow_variant: before:absolute before:inset-0 before:rounded-2xl before:bg-gradient-to-br before:from-white/[0.08] before:to-transparent before:pointer-events-none

WorkflowList.card_style: glass panel, 12px gap between cards, selected state: accent primary border glow
WorkflowList.trigger_badge: pill shape, glass fill, icon + label
WorkflowList.empty_state: centered floating orb animation + 'No workflows yet' in text.muted

step_nodes.style: horizontal timeline with connecting lines, nodes are 36px circles
step_nodes.running: animated pulse ring in accent.primary, inner dot
step_nodes.failed: accent.danger with shake micro-animation on mount

WorkflowDetail.expand_detail: AnimatePresence slide-down card below the node, glass panel
WorkflowDetail.re_run_button: glass button, accent.primary border, hover: glow + scale(1.02)

mic_button.recording: pulsing 3-ring animation in accent.primary, scale bounces on audio peaks
mic_button.waveform: 5 animated bars with spring physics height based on audio amplitude

VoiceInput.transcript: glass textarea, mono font, character count in corner
VoiceInput.intent_chips: horizontal scroll row, glass pills, icon + label
VoiceInput.generate_button: full-width glass button, gradient border accent.primary → accent.secondary, disabled state: opacity-40
VoiceInput.fallback_textarea: same glass style but with keyboard icon instead of mic

check_row.pass: left border accent.success, checkmark icon, fade-in on mount
check_row.fail: left border accent.danger, X icon, subtle red bg tint

ValidationPanel.approve_button: gradient fill accent.primary → accent.secondary, white text, ripple on click
ValidationPanel.overall_verdict: large pill badge top of panel, animated score meter

EventStreamFeed.event_row: slide-in from right (AnimatePresence), monospace timestamp, color-coded event type pill
EventStreamFeed.scroll_behavior: auto-scroll to bottom, pause on hover, 'scroll to latest' button appears when paused

codex_instruction.task: Generate the complete /workflows page and all 6 sub-components as described. Use Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion. Implement Spline 3D background. Apply every glass panel, color token, animation, and component spec above exactly. Do NOT simplify. Do NOT use minimal design. Every component must feel premium, spatial, and graphically rich.
constraints: Spline scene loads lazily with React Suspense fallback

model_recommendation.reasoning: GPT-4o via Codex excels at following dense JSON design specs and producing pixel-accurate component code. It handles the Spline integration, Framer Motion animation specs, and Tailwind v4 glass morphism patterns reliably when given a structured prompt like this one.
model_recommendation.alternative: Claude Sonnet 4.6 — better for architectural consistency and making sure the component interacts correctly with the existing AgentForge event bus and API patterns. Use Sonnet for the backend wiring, GPT-4o/Codex for the visual layer.
model_recommendation.recommended_workflow: 1. Feed this JSON to Codex → get rich visual components. 2. Pass those components to Claude Sonnet → wire up the API calls, useAgentEvents hook, and type safety. 3. Spline scene: design at spline.design, export as viewer URL, embed with @splinetool/react-spline.
